title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 Contract · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs node 24, typescript 7, and amqplib 2.0.1 contract from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed amqplib lifecycle and boundary contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Choose one amqplib promise or callback API and account for RPC methods, event emitters, callback-only confirms, and non-RPC methods.
Treat delivered bytes, fields, properties, headers, and decoded payload as untrusted runtime data despite TypeScript declarations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Runtime Client Version · changed case · Review Runtime Client Version
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 3153 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a typed amqplib client without treating types or publish booleans as broker proof; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18, npm 12, TypeScript 7, amqplib 2.0.1, AMQP 0-9-1, and RabbitMQ 4.3.2 as one reviewed compatibility contract.

Which action demonstrates Runtime Client Version without dropping earlier contracts?
